channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: FLASHBACK: Unearthed photo shows smiling Obama touring SpaceX alongside 'Nazi nepo baby' Elon Musk
item.description: Before Elon Musk waded into the political arena with the Department of Government Efficiency, becoming a target of Democrats, he was considered an Obama ally.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dem congressman lashes Musk in opening salvo of populist bid in 2026 swing-state Senate race
item.description: Rep. Chris Pappas, D-N.H., on Thursday declared his candidacy in the 2026 race to succeed retiring Sen. Jeanne Shaheen for a key battleground state seat Republicans are eyeing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump moves to fire several national security officials over concerns they're not loyal: AP sources
item.description: President Donald Trump has moved to fire several senior White House National Security Council officials, acting soon after being urged by far-right activist Laura Loomer to purge staffers she deemed insufficiently committed to his agenda.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Aamer Madhani And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: EEOC chief shifts focus to investigating DEI but the methods provoke an outcry
item.description: The acting chief of the top federal agency for protecting worker rights has signaled a pivot toward prioritizing President Donald Trump’s campaign to stamp out diversity, equity and inclusion programs in the private and public sectors.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Olson And Claire Savage, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Justice Department declined to prosecute Texas AG Paxton in final weeks of Biden's term: AP sources
item.description: The Justice Department quietly decided in the final weeks of the Biden administration not to prosecute Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton, effectively ending the corruption investigation that cast a long shadow over the political career of a close Republican ally of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer And Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: White House denies reports that Elon Musk is being pushed out
item.description: A report from Politico claims Trump has told his inner circle that Musk will soon take his leave from the White House.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: Ontario premier ‘cautiously optimistic’ amid Trump tariffs
item.description: “Make no mistake about it, Canadians love Americans. They love the U.S., and I do, too,” Doug Ford said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Scott Walker: 'Big money' won Susan Crawford a Supreme Court seat
item.description: Crawford defeated conservative Brad Schimel by 10 points in the officially nonpartisan race, cementing a liberal majority on the court.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Trump announces sweeping 'reciprocal' tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump announced plans for sweeping tariffs Wednesday, saying “our country has been looted, pillaged, raped, plundered” by other nations.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court sides with the FDA in its dispute over sweet-flavored vaping products
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday ruled for the Food and Drug Administration in its crackdown on sweet-flavored vaping products following a surge in teen electronic cigarette use.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No bathroom breaks: How Booker accomplished record Senate speech
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) set a new record for the longest Senate speech in history on Monday and Tuesday by holding the upper chamber’s floor for more than 25 hours.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Proposed cut in local aid to prevent aquatic invasive species sparks fears of more infested lakes
item.description: For the past decade, Minnesota has distributed about $10 million a year to counties for aquatic invasive species prevention. Gov. Tim Walz’s proposed budget reduced that amount by 50 percent. Some lake advocates worry cutting the funding could potentially lead to more infested waters.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Eric Adams federal bribery case dismissed
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) -- Mayor Eric Adams' federal bribery case has been dismissed, court records show. In a letter filed Wednesday, Judge Dale Ho ordered the case dropped with prejudice, meaning the government cannot bring these charges against Adams again in the future.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: State Sen. Napoleon Harris wins Thornton Township supervisor seat, Jason House victorious in Dolton mayoral race
item.description: Village of Dolton Senior Trustee Jason House won the election for Dolton mayor by a landslide, while State Sen. Napoleon B. Harris III claimed victory to become the Thornton Township Supervisor-elect, unseating the incumbent, self-proclaimed “Super Mayor” Tiffany Henyard.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Spinelli, WGN 9

item.title: White House says it’s ’case closed’ on the Signal group chat review
item.description: Last week, the White House said the National Security Council, the White House counsel office and President Trump adviser Elon Musk were all looking into the mishap.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Luna accuses House Freedom Caucus of smear; exits group over parental proxy voting battle
item.description: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-Fla.) announced Monday that she is leaving the House Freedom Caucus amid her battle with GOP leadership and hard-line Republicans over her push to allow proxy voting for new parents.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Crime, housing and Cuomo in focus in race for NYC mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Candidates on the campaign trail for New York City mayor are zeroing in on crime and affordability, as a new accusation against former Gov. Andrew Cuomo bubbles to the surface.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Trump says he’s ‘not joking’ about a third term. Republicans insist he is
item.description: Congressional Republicans are brushing off President Trump’s latest comments about seeking a third term, downplaying them as a joke designed to needle the media even as the president insists he is serious.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mayor Adams' lawyer urges ‘prompt resolution’ ahead of election deadline
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – With a filing deadline approaching in New York City’s upcoming mayoral election, Mayor Eric Adams’ lawyer has urged a judge to "promptly" issue a decision in the mayor’s bribery case. Petitions are due this week for candidates to appear on a party's primary ballot.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Trump tariff tumult has ripples for sporting goods, puts costly hockey gear in price-hike crosshair
item.description: The sports equipment industry is no exception to the headaches being caused throughout the U.S. commercial ecosystem by the promise of 25 percent tariffs on Canadian imports by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Illinois to defy Trump voter order for April election
item.description: President Trump earlier this week issued an executive order calling for all voters to provide proof of citizenship before receiving a ballot.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: California Assembly Democrats announce plans to leave X over disinformation, hate speech
item.description: California Assembly Democrats announced plans to leave X over disinformation, lack of content moderation and hate speech.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Here are the international students and faculty known to be targeted by ICE
item.description: The Trump administration’s crackdown on foreign students and faculty who have voiced support for the Palestinian cause has escalated, with universities across the country seeing students either arrested by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or having their visas revoked.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5
